---
title: "Monday Catch-Up"
date: "2026-05-04"
categories:
  - Planning
  - Mussel Methylation
  - Mussel Biomarkers
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false

editor:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---

### Plan of the Week: May 4 - May 10, 2026

#### *High- level outline for the week. Adjusted daily to reflect progress of the day before*

-   This week is all about getting biomarkers out!

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<div>

> Monday - Catching up & weekly planning
>
> Tuesday - Steven Working Session & UW-RUA
>
> Wednesday - Biomarkers & UG Bench Prep
>
> Thursday - Biomarkers & Bench Work
>
> Friday - Biomarkers & Bench
>
> Saturday - Biomarkers
>
> Sunday - Afternoon Methylation Work

</div>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Plan of the Day

### *Granular level task list to accomplish the high- level goal outlined above*

-   My goals for today are:
    -   Wrap-up my GSAR and Steven 1v1 Agenda for Tuesday
    -   Continue the methylation results and discussion framing
    -   Plan T-F with specific tasks

### Projects Touched Today

-   Mussel Methylation
-   Mussel Biomarkers

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Progress Notes

-   Mondays are email/ task/ planning days
    -   I took a look at my upcoming deliverables, knocked out comms,
        and started building a plan for the week. My primary focus is on
        getting my mussel manuscripts out for review and editing.
    -   I also have a flurry of tasks for the last UW-RUA professional
        development programming event that takes place tomorrow.
-   I looked at what is on the agenda for tomorrow with Steven and
    started by knocking out my half of the GSAR. Not a morale booster,
    that's for sure.
    -   Turning to biomarkers - I added the notebook post with updated
        figures.
    -   I spent time writing out specific tasks to update the writing.
    -   Shifting to methylation, I took my handwritten notes and added a
        lose framing to both the results and discussion sections.
    -   I added the progress for 2026-27 AY funding options.
    -   Added the update on getting Cole into the lab.
-   I shifted over to knocking out UW-RUA tasks for tomorrow before
    meeting with Yen-Chu to discuss the PoE teaching positions at the
    end of the day.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Outcomes: Products & Word Count

-   Drafting methylation: 386 words

<div>

> **Today's total: 386 words**
>
> **Monthly total to date: 386 words**
>
> **Annual total to date: 41,548 words**
>
> **Annual target total to date: 60,000 words**

</div>

### Next Up: Tomorrow's Plan

-   Will be made during weekly set-up.
